scenes.callout: Activity is not the same as judgment
scenes.chapterIntro: Replace prompt performance with four observable human decisions
chapterSummary: Measure decisions, not usage volume
Use the four human skills as one loop
Move from intent to evidence without losing judgment
scenes.narration: AI fluency can look impressive and still produce bad work. Someone writes elaborate prompts, uses AI all day, and accepts a polished answer in seconds. The activity looks advanced. The judgment may be missing. The better measure is not how often you use AI. It is whether you can decide when to involve it, explain the job clearly, evaluate what comes back, and take responsibility for what happens next.

scenes.callout: Four human skills form AI fluency
scenes.chapterIntro: Replace prompt performance with four observable human decisions
chapterSummary: Measure decisions, not usage volume
Use the four human skills as one loop
Move from intent to evidence without losing judgment
scenes.narration: Those four abilities form the 4D AI Fluency Framework: Delegation, Description, Discernment, and Diligence. They are human skills. A stronger model can help, but it cannot remove the need for them.

scenes.callout: Move from intent to evidence
scenes.chapterIntro: Replace prompt performance with four observable human decisions
chapterSummary: Measure decisions, not usage volume
Use the four human skills as one loop
Move from intent to evidence without losing judgment
scenes.narration: In this video, we will turn the four dimensions into a practical loop. You will learn the decision behind each one, the common failure it prevents, and a reusable self-check for your next real task. Keep one idea in view: fluency is not a performance of clever prompting. It is the ability to move from intent to evidence without giving up judgment.

semanticBeats.trigger: In this video, we will turn

scenes.callout: The first decision comes before the prompt
scenes.chapterIntro: Choose whether, where, and how AI should participate before writing the prompt
chapterSummary: decide whether AI belongs in the task before writing the prompt
delegate a bounded outcome, not a vague responsibility
keep human authority where consequence and judgment live
scenes.narration: Delegation comes before the prompt. It means setting the goal and deciding whether, where, and how AI should participate. The first question is not, “What should I ask?” It is, “What part of this work should AI do at all?” Start from the job, not the tool. Name the decision, artifact, or change you need. Then ask which parts require your context, taste, authority, or relationship with other people, and which parts are repeatable enough for AI assistance.

scenes.callout: Route work by judgment and evidence
scenes.chapterIntro: Choose whether, where, and how AI should participate before writing the prompt
chapterSummary: decide whether AI belongs in the task before writing the prompt
delegate a bounded outcome, not a vague responsibility
keep human authority where consequence and judgment live
scenes.narration: Use three routes. Keep the task human when the value is personal judgment or accountability. Collaborate when the destination is clear but the route needs frequent choices. Delegate when the outcome, tools, limits, and evidence can be specified. Good delegation also defines the outcome boundary. State what success looks like, what the AI may access, which actions are allowed, when it must stop, and what evidence it must return. Autonomy without a boundary is just hidden interpretation.

scenes.callout: Speed does not make a task ready
scenes.chapterIntro: Choose whether, where, and how AI should participate before writing the prompt
chapterSummary: decide whether AI belongs in the task before writing the prompt
delegate a bounded outcome, not a vague responsibility
keep human authority where consequence and judgment live
scenes.narration: Consider an important email. AI can draft alternatives or improve clarity, but you may keep the decision, relationship tone, and final send. For a routine weekly report, the AI may collect data and assemble the artifact while you review exceptions and approve publication. The common failure is over-delegation. People transfer work because AI is fast, not because the task is ready. If the goal is unstable, the consequence is high, or success cannot be checked, stay in collaboration or do the judgment yourself.

scenes.callout: Recap
scenes.chapterIntro: Choose whether, where, and how AI should participate before writing the prompt
chapterSummary: decide whether AI belongs in the task before writing the prompt
delegate a bounded outcome, not a vague responsibility
keep human authority where consequence and judgment live
scenes.narration: First, decide whether AI belongs in the task before writing the prompt. Second, delegate a bounded outcome, not a vague responsibility. Third, keep human authority where consequence and judgment live.

scenes.callout: Make the hidden standard visible
scenes.chapterIntro: Turn private intent into a brief another session can execute without guessing
chapterSummary: translate intent into an outcome, context, constraints, and evidence
describe the tradeoff the AI must preserve
prefer a small high-signal brief over a long noisy prompt
scenes.narration: Description turns private intent into an observable brief. AI cannot reliably infer the audience in your head, the tradeoff you care about, or the standard you will use to reject the result. If those things matter, make them visible. A high-signal description contains five parts: the desired outcome, the relevant context, the audience or user, the hard constraints, and the evidence or test that defines success. Examples help when they reveal a preference, not when they merely add more text.

scenes.callout: Describe decisions, not decoration
scenes.chapterIntro: Turn private intent into a brief another session can execute without guessing
chapterSummary: translate intent into an outcome, context, constraints, and evidence
describe the tradeoff the AI must preserve
prefer a small high-signal brief over a long noisy prompt
scenes.narration: Describe tradeoffs instead of adjectives. “Make it professional” leaves many interpretations. “Write for a skeptical engineering lead, keep the claim narrow, show the operational cost, and avoid marketing language” gives the system decisions it can act on. Imagine asking for a launch plan. A weak description says, “Create a great launch strategy.” A useful one names the product stage, target user, available channels, budget, prohibited claims, required deliverables, and the metric that will determine whether the experiment continues.

scenes.callout: Remove context that cannot change the decision
scenes.chapterIntro: Turn private intent into a brief another session can execute without guessing
chapterSummary: translate intent into an outcome, context, constraints, and evidence
describe the tradeoff the AI must preserve
prefer a small high-signal brief over a long noisy prompt
scenes.narration: Description is not a one-time prompt ritual. During collaboration, update the shared artifact and acceptance criteria as you learn. During delegation, package the final state so another agent or another session can resume without reconstructing the conversation. The common failure is context dumping. More context can hide the signal. Remove history that does not change the decision, label uncertain facts, separate requirements from suggestions, and put the most important constraint where it cannot be missed.

semanticBeats.trigger: Description is not a one-time prompt ritual

scenes.callout: Recap
scenes.chapterIntro: Turn private intent into a brief another session can execute without guessing
chapterSummary: translate intent into an outcome, context, constraints, and evidence
describe the tradeoff the AI must preserve
prefer a small high-signal brief over a long noisy prompt
scenes.narration: First, translate intent into an outcome, context, constraints, and evidence. Second, describe the tradeoff the AI must preserve. Third, prefer a small high-signal brief over a long noisy prompt.

scenes.callout: Fluency is not proof
scenes.chapterIntro: Evaluate task fit, evidence, and consequence instead of trusting fluent language
chapterSummary: judge task fit before judging presentation quality
separate evidence, inference, and unresolved uncertainty
add friction where an error would change a real decision
scenes.narration: Discernment is the ability to assess the usefulness of AI outputs and behavior. Fluent language is not proof. A result can be coherent, complete-looking, and still be wrong, irrelevant, or unsafe for the situation. Evaluate at three levels. First, does the artifact answer the actual task? Second, are important claims supported by reliable evidence? Third, does the result fit the audience, constraints, and consequence of use? Passing one level does not guarantee the others.

scenes.callout: Create evidence you can discriminate between
scenes.chapterIntro: Evaluate task fit, evidence, and consequence instead of trusting fluent language
chapterSummary: judge task fit before judging presentation quality
separate evidence, inference, and unresolved uncertainty
add friction where an error would change a real decision
scenes.narration: Inspect assumptions, omissions, and confidence boundaries. Ask what would make the conclusion false, which source supports the key claim, and which part is an inference. A useful answer should make important uncertainty easier to see, not easier to ignore. Create contrast when judgment is difficult. Request two approaches with explicit tradeoffs, test the result against an example it may fail on, or compare the output with a primary source. Evaluation improves when there is something concrete to discriminate between.

semanticBeats.trigger: Inspect assumptions, omissions, and confidence boundaries

scenes.callout: Do not stop at the first smooth answer
scenes.chapterIntro: Evaluate task fit, evidence, and consequence instead of trusting fluent language
chapterSummary: judge task fit before judging presentation quality
separate evidence, inference, and unresolved uncertainty
add friction where an error would change a real decision
scenes.narration: For a research brief, do not grade the number of links. Check whether the sources are primary, whether the dates fit the question, whether quoted evidence supports the conclusion, and whether unresolved uncertainty is named. A confident summary without that chain is not ready. The common failure is accepting the first smooth answer. Speed creates a false stopping signal. Pause where the decision is expensive, where a claim changes action, or where the artifact will be published, sent, merged, purchased, or used to judge another person.

scenes.callout: Recap
scenes.chapterIntro: Evaluate task fit, evidence, and consequence instead of trusting fluent language
chapterSummary: judge task fit before judging presentation quality
separate evidence, inference, and unresolved uncertainty
add friction where an error would change a real decision
scenes.narration: First, judge task fit before judging presentation quality. Second, separate evidence, inference, and unresolved uncertainty. Third, add friction where an error would change a real decision.

scenes.callout: The user owns the decision to rely on AI
scenes.chapterIntro: Keep responsibility, permissions, and real-world verification attached to the action
chapterSummary: keep a record of permissions, checks, and the final decision
match AI authority to reversibility and consequence
verify the outcome after the tool reports success
scenes.narration: Diligence means taking responsibility for how AI is used and what happens with its output. The model may generate the material, but the user still owns the decision to rely on it, share it, execute it, or place it in someone else’s path. Start with an audit trail. Record the goal, important inputs, permissions, checks, and final human decision. For sensitive work, also track data handling, rights, attribution, and who approved the consequential step. Memory is not a control system.

scenes.callout: Authority rises only with evidence
scenes.chapterIntro: Keep responsibility, permissions, and real-world verification attached to the action
chapterSummary: keep a record of permissions, checks, and the final decision
match AI authority to reversibility and consequence
verify the outcome after the tool reports success
scenes.narration: Use a permission ladder. Reading and drafting are usually easier to reverse. Editing shared data, sending messages, publishing content, spending money, or deleting records creates larger consequences. The authority granted to AI should rise only with stronger evidence and explicit gates. Define stop conditions before execution. The agent should pause when a required fact is missing, a permission changes, a cost crosses the limit, a test fails, or the task expands beyond the original scope. A good stop is a sign of control, not a failure of autonomy.

scenes.callout: Check the world after the call returns
scenes.chapterIntro: Keep responsibility, permissions, and real-world verification attached to the action
chapterSummary: keep a record of permissions, checks, and the final decision
match AI authority to reversibility and consequence
verify the outcome after the tool reports success
scenes.narration: Consider publishing a video. Rendering a local draft can run automatically. Public release requires verified media, metadata, visibility, and channel placement. A protected declaration or final platform confirmation stays with the human because it carries identity and accountability. The common failure is mistaking tool success for outcome success. An upload request can return successfully while the video is private, missing its thumbnail, or outside the intended playlist. Diligence verifies the real-world state after the action.

scenes.callout: Recap
scenes.chapterIntro: Keep responsibility, permissions, and real-world verification attached to the action
chapterSummary: keep a record of permissions, checks, and the final decision
match AI authority to reversibility and consequence
verify the outcome after the tool reports success
scenes.narration: First, keep a record of permissions, checks, and the final decision. Second, match AI authority to reversibility and consequence. Third, verify the outcome after the tool reports success.

scenes.callout: Each decision creates the next test
scenes.chapterIntro: Run the four dimensions as a chain and return to the real source of failure
chapterSummary: treat Delegation, Description, Discernment, and Diligence as one chain
return to the dimension that caused the failure
preserve state and evidence so the loop can resume
scenes.narration: The four dimensions are not separate personality traits. They form a working loop. Delegation chooses the right job. Description makes the job legible. Discernment evaluates the result. Diligence governs how the result is used. Each dimension creates input for the next. A clear delegation boundary tells you what to describe. A precise description creates acceptance criteria for discernment. Discernment reveals uncertainty. Diligence decides whether to revise, approve, stop, or change the boundary.

semanticBeats.trigger: The four dimensions are not separate

scenes.callout: Repair the weakest dimension
scenes.chapterIntro: Run the four dimensions as a chain and return to the real source of failure
chapterSummary: treat Delegation, Description, Discernment, and Diligence as one chain
return to the dimension that caused the failure
preserve state and evidence so the loop can resume
scenes.narration: Then the loop runs again. If the result fails because the goal was vague, return to Delegation. If the AI misunderstood a constraint, improve Description. If a claim is unsupported, strengthen Discernment. If the action exceeded authority, repair Diligence. The weakest dimension often determines the outcome. Excellent description cannot rescue the wrong task. Strong delegation cannot rescue careless evaluation. Perfect evidence cannot justify an unauthorized action. Fluency requires the whole chain.

scenes.callout: Resume from evidence, not conversation history
scenes.chapterIntro: Run the four dimensions as a chain and return to the real source of failure
chapterSummary: treat Delegation, Description, Discernment, and Diligence as one chain
return to the dimension that caused the failure
preserve state and evidence so the loop can resume
scenes.narration: This loop is especially useful when work is interrupted. Save the current goal, artifact state, unresolved question, next allowed action, and acceptance test. A new session can resume from evidence instead of replaying a long conversation. Use the loop after real tasks, not as a vague self-rating. Ask which decision was hardest, where rework appeared, what evidence changed your mind, and which control prevented a mistake. Improvement becomes visible when it changes the next workflow.

scenes.callout: Recap
scenes.chapterIntro: Run the four dimensions as a chain and return to the real source of failure
chapterSummary: treat Delegation, Description, Discernment, and Diligence as one chain
return to the dimension that caused the failure
preserve state and evidence so the loop can resume
scenes.narration: First, treat Delegation, Description, Discernment, and Diligence as one chain. Second, return to the dimension that caused the failure. Third, preserve state and evidence so the loop can resume.

scenes.callout: Score the task, not your identity
scenes.chapterIntro: Score the current task and repair every zero before expanding AI authority
chapterSummary: score the task from zero to two on each of the four dimensions
treat any zero as a reason to stop and repair the workflow
expand AI authority only after all four dimensions are testable
scenes.narration: Now turn the framework into a 4D AI Fluency Card. Score the current task, not your identity. The purpose is to find the weak link before you give AI more context, tools, or authority. Use a simple scale: zero means the decision is missing, one means it is partly explicit, and two means it is clear and testable. Do not hide a zero inside a high total. Any missing dimension can block the workflow.

scenes.callout: Can another session act?
scenes.chapterIntro: Score the current task and repair every zero before expanding AI authority
chapterSummary: score the task from zero to two on each of the four dimensions
treat any zero as a reason to stop and repair the workflow
expand AI authority only after all four dimensions are testable
scenes.narration: For Delegation, ask: Is the desired outcome specific? Have I chosen what AI does and what remains human? Are tools, permissions, stop conditions, and handoff evidence bounded? If not, keep the task in collaboration. For Description, ask: Does the brief include relevant context, audience, constraints, examples only where useful, and an acceptance test? Could another session act on it without guessing what “good” means?

scenes.callout: A good average cannot hide a zero
scenes.chapterIntro: Score the current task and repair every zero before expanding AI authority
chapterSummary: score the task from zero to two on each of the four dimensions
treat any zero as a reason to stop and repair the workflow
expand AI authority only after all four dimensions are testable
scenes.narration: For Discernment, ask: Have I checked task fit, important claims, assumptions, and uncertainty? Did I compare evidence or test a failure case where the result matters? Polished language earns no automatic point. For Diligence, ask: Is the action reversible, authorized, recorded, and verified afterward? Do I know who owns the final decision? Before consequential execution, require two points in every dimension, not merely a good average.

scenes.callout: Recap
scenes.chapterIntro: Score the current task and repair every zero before expanding AI authority
chapterSummary: score the task from zero to two on each of the four dimensions
treat any zero as a reason to stop and repair the workflow
expand AI authority only after all four dimensions are testable
scenes.narration: First, score the task from zero to two on each of the four dimensions. Second, treat any zero as a reason to stop and repair the workflow. Third, expand AI authority only after all four dimensions are testable.

scenes.callout: Repair every zero before expanding authority
scenes.chapterIntro: Use the 4D card before the next consequential AI task
chapterSummary: Delegation decides whether, where, and how AI should participate
Description makes the goal, context, constraints, and evidence explicit
Discernment tests the output, while Diligence controls and verifies its use
scenes.narration: AI fluency is not measured by prompt complexity, daily usage, or how quickly you accept an answer. It is the quality of the decisions that connect your intent to a real outcome. First, Delegation decides whether, where, and how AI should participate. Second, Description makes the goal, context, constraints, and evidence explicit. Third, Discernment tests the output, while Diligence controls and verifies its use. For your next task, use the 4D card before execution. Repair every zero, keep the acceptance test visible, and let evidence determine whether the AI receives more authority. The models will change. The need to choose the right work, describe it clearly, judge results, and own consequences will remain. Those are the skills that make stronger AI genuinely useful. Follow Tiny Agent. Tiny Agent helps you get better at using AI.
